packs.description: Limit access to systems holding EU personal data to staff with a processing purpose, and deny broad/default access in line with data-minimisation and integrity & confidentiality.
packs.name: GDPR — personal data least privilege

templates.name: Data team → customer PII store
templates.summary: Grant the data team access to the customer PII database for defined processing.

templates.name: Deny-all to customer PII by default
templates.summary: Default-deny the whole organisation to the PII store; grant back by purpose.

packs.authority: U.S. HHS Office for Civil Rights
packs.description: Role-based access to electronic protected health information (ePHI): clinical staff get access to patient records, everyone else is denied, and break-glass is reserved for on-call responders.
packs.name: HIPAA Security Rule — ePHI access

templates.name: Clinical staff → patient records
templates.summary: Grant clinicians read/write access to the EHR system.

templates.name: Deny non-clinical staff to ePHI
templates.summary: Deny general/administrative staff direct access to patient records.

templates.name: Break-glass — on-call responders
templates.summary: Grant the incident-response on-call group emergency access to ePHI (audited, simulate before promoting).

packs.description: Annex A.5 access-control baseline: business-need-driven access to information systems, restricted privileged utilities, and a default-deny posture.
packs.name: ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A — access control

templates.name: Business need-to-know access
templates.summary: Grant a department access to its own line-of-business application.

templates.control: ISO 27001 A.8.18 — Use of privileged utility programs
templates.name: Restrict privileged utility access
templates.summary: Grant only the sysadmin role access to privileged admin tooling.

templates.name: Default-deny information systems
templates.summary: Deny everyone by default; grant access by business need.

packs.description: Least-privilege access to the cardholder data environment (CDE): restrict who can reach systems storing or processing cardholder data, and keep contractors and general staff out by default.
packs.name: PCI-DSS v4.0 — Cardholder Data Environment

templates.control: PCI-DSS 7.2 — Restrict access by business need-to-know
templates.name: CDE administration — payments team only
templates.summary: Grant the payments operations team admin access to the cardholder data environment.

templates.name: Block contractors from the CDE
templates.summary: Deny all contractor identities access to cardholder systems.

templates.name: Deny-all to cardholder data by default
templates.summary: Default-deny everyone to the cardholder database; grant back explicitly.

packs.description: Common-Criteria logical access: production systems restricted to engineering/SRE, privileged access limited to admins, and default-deny for the rest.
packs.name: SOC 2 — logical access controls

templates.name: Engineering → production apps
templates.summary: Grant engineering access to production application systems.

templates.name: Privileged prod access — admins only
templates.summary: Grant the platform-admin group admin access to production infrastructure.

templates.name: Deny-all to production by default
templates.summary: Default-deny the organisation to production hosts; grant back per role.
